description:
You are working as a data scientist who has just received a small customer dataset that contains a few rows with the literal string “NULL” in one or more columns.  
Your goal is to remove every data row that contains the exact substring “NULL” in any column and save the cleaned result to a new file.

Dataset location (already present):
  • /home/user/data/raw/customers.csv

Exact, original file contents:  
(customer_id,first_name,last_name,email,age — header line followed by 6 rows)

customer_id,first_name,last_name,email,age  
1,John,Doe,john.doe@example.com,28  
2,Jane,NULL,jane@example.com,34  
3,Bob,Smith,NULL,45  
4,Anna,Adams,anna.adams@example.com,31  
5,NULL,White,white@example.com,27  
6,Mark,Brown,mark.brown@example.com,NULL  

Required final output:
  1. Create the directory /home/user/data/clean (if it does not already exist).
  2. Inside that directory, create a file named customers_clean.csv whose exact,
     complete contents must be (including the trailing newline at the end of the last line):

customer_id,first_name,last_name,email,age  
1,John,Doe,john.doe@example.com,28  
4,Anna,Adams,anna.adams@example.com,31  

No additional blank lines or spaces are allowed anywhere in the file.  
Other than the single cleaned CSV file, nothing else should be created or modified.

Important constraints:
  • Accomplish the row-removal in one single shell command (you may use shell pipelines).  
  • Do not manually open the file and edit it—perform the cleaning via command-line text processing utilities of your choice (e.g., awk, grep, csvkit, etc.).
  • Do not produce any terminal output that would break automated checking—the testing harness verifies only the presence and exact contents of /home/user/data/clean/customers_clean.csv.

When you are finished, the only new artefact should be the cleaned CSV located exactly at:
  /home/user/data/clean/customers_clean.csv
truth:
Pre-existing file before the task starts:
/home/user/data/raw/customers.csv  (permissions 644, owned by user:user)
Its contents are precisely:

customer_id,first_name,last_name,email,age
1,John,Doe,john.doe@example.com,28
2,Jane,NULL,jane@example.com,34
3,Bob,Smith,NULL,45
4,Anna,Adams,anna.adams@example.com,31
5,NULL,White,white@example.com,27
6,Mark,Brown,mark.brown@example.com,NULL

Directory /home/user/data/clean does not initially exist.

File that must exist after successful task completion:
/home/user/data/clean/customers_clean.csv  (permissions 644, owned by user:user)
Exact required contents (including final newline):

customer_id,first_name,last_name,email,age
1,John,Doe,john.doe@example.com,28
4,Anna,Adams,anna.adams@example.com,31

No other files or directories should be created, deleted, or modified.
